The date the vendor payable was approved for payment. Marks the transition from pending review to authorized for payment processing.
The user identifier of the person who approved the vendor payable for payment. Required for financial controls and audit trail compliance.
The timestamp when this vendor payable record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payable amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange management.
The discount amount available if payment is made by the early payment discount deadline. Supports cash management optimization through early payment incentives.
The last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount. Used for cash flow optimization decisions.
The date the payable was flagged as disputed. Used for aging analysis of unresolved disputes and vendor relationship management.
Free-text description of the reason for disputing the vendor payable. Populated when payment_status is disputed. Used for vendor negotiation and dispute tracking.
The date by which payment must be made to the vendor according to the agreed payment terms. Used for cash flow planning and aging analysis.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which this vendor payable is recorded. Used for period-based financial reporting and variance analysis.
The fiscal year in which this vendor payable is recorded for financial reporting purposes. Derived from posting date and fiscal calendar configuration.
The total amount owed to the vendor before any discounts or adjustments are applied. Represents the full invoice value.
The date the vendor invoice was issued. This is the principal business event timestamp for the payable transaction and serves as the baseline for payment term calculations.
The unique payable reference identifier from Mediaocean Prisma billing system. Enables reconciliation between media buying platform and financial system for media cost tracking.
The timestamp when this vendor payable record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
The final amount payable to the vendor after applying early payment discounts and any other adjustments. This is the actual payment amount.
Free-text notes or comments related to the vendor payable. Used for documenting special payment instructions, reconciliation notes, or other relevant information.
The unique business document number assigned to this accounts payable record, used for tracking and reconciliation purposes.
The date the payment was actually made to the vendor. Null for unpaid payables. Used for cash flow reporting and payment performance analysis.

The payment instrument or mechanism used to settle the vendor payable. ACH (Automated Clearing House) for electronic bank transfers, wire transfer for same-day payments, check for paper-based payments, credit card for card-based payments, EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) for direct bank transfers, virtual card for single-use card payments.
The unique reference number assigned to the payment transaction (e.g., check number, wire confirmation number, ACH trace number). Used for payment tracking and bank reconciliation.

Current status of the vendor payment in the accounts payable lifecycle. Open indicates unpaid liability, scheduled indicates payment is queued, paid indicates payment completed, disputed indicates vendor dispute in progress, cancelled indicates payable voided, on_hold indicates payment temporarily suspended.
The contractual payment terms agreed with the vendor, typically expressed as net days (net_30, net_60, net_90, net_120). Determines the due date calculation from invoice date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Configuration

description: The date the vendor payable was posted to the general ledger. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting and may differ from invoice date for period-end accruals.

Status of the three-way reconciliation process between vendor invoice, media buy order, and actual delivery. Pending indicates awaiting reconciliation, matched indicates full agreement, discrepancy indicates variance requiring investigation, resolved indicates discrepancy closed, escalated indicates dispute requiring management intervention.

business_glossary_term: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) Vendor Invoice Document Number
description: The SAP S/4HANA FI document number for the vendor invoice posting. Links the payable to the general ledger entry and enables full financial audit trail.

Indicates whether this payable is subject to sequential liability rules, where the agency is liable for vendor payment only after receiving payment from the client. Critical for cash flow management and risk mitigation in media buying.
reference: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Sequential Liability Guidelines

description: The total tax amount included in the vendor payable (e.g., sales tax, VAT, GST). Required for tax reporting and compliance with jurisdictional tax regulations.
The amount of tax withheld from the vendor payment as required by tax regulations. Reduces the net payment amount and must be remitted to tax authorities.
